subject: Sports
title: Nadia Comaneci's Perfect 10
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Nadia Comaneci's Perfect 10.

hook.text: The scoreboard couldn't display her score. It had never needed to show a perfect 10 before.

buildup.text: At Montreal 1976, 14-year-old Romanian gymnast Nadia Comaneci performed on the uneven bars.

discovery.text: Her routine was flawless. Judges awarded 10.0—the first perfect score in Olympic gymnastics history.

twist.text: The scoreboard showed 1.00 because it only had three digits—it wasn't built for perfection.

climax.text: Comaneci earned seven perfect 10s at those Games and won three gold medals.

punchline.text: She broke a scoreboard that never imagined perfection.

quiz.question: Why did the scoreboard show 1.00 for Nadia's score?
options: It couldn't display 10.0 with only three digits
It was a different scoring system
